name: Norman Granz
abstract: Norman Granz (August 6, 1918 – November 22, 2001) was an American jazz record producer, concert promoter, and founder. He founded the record labels Clef, Norgran, Down Home, Verve, and Pablo and the Jazz at the Philharmonic concert series.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: The MJQ briefly hooked up with Norman Granz and the Jazz at the Philharmonic... he wanted the Quartet on there... we had to do a record on Norman Granz’s label, Verve or whatever it is... we had to put an album on Pablo in order for Norman to release Milt
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: Norman Granz was connected with Chico O'Farrill... Mario Bauza and Charlie Parker and Norman Granz we got together... he took Charlie Parker to listen to the band to see if he liked to record with us. So he liked.

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: my first recording, which was done by Norman Granz... Norman Granz brought JATP [Jazz at the Philharmonic] to Japan and played... I really didn‟t think I could play... But once we started playing it was okay.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Norman Granz came into our lives with his whole Jazz at the Philharmonic enterprise... The one incident... that got a lot of attention for the two of us... was a successful seller for Norman and for us.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Oscar Peterson
about_quotes.evidence: Norman had the facility to listen to various players and pair them... I remember we were listening to one player... he'd say, 'boy he can play. I'd like to hear him with Little Jazz'
about_quotes.doc_title: Oscar Peterson interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I made up a coupla songs 'bout three...four weeks ago for Norman Granz. Me and Eddie Vinson "Cleanhead" was out there doin' a session with Count Basie so we done a coupla numbers.
